mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in monthly employment growth signals weakening aggregate demand, prompting equity investors to price in lower corporate profit margins and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 50.0 thousand
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Diverging trends between industrial production and housing starts indicate a structural supply-demand imbalance that increases input cost volatility, forcing equity valuations lower over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve model R-squared by at least 0.02 over a baseline single-proxy model, or forward returns show no significant spread between high-divergence and low-divergence regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation combined with rising policy rates triggers a repricing of long-duration assets, reducing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for historical volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in real consumer credit outstanding reflects expanding household purchasing power, which directly supports corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The series exhibits a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with forward equity returns across three consecutive rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial output reflects supply chain disruptions or capacity constraints that compress corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns within a one-month window.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant negative skew during triggered periods, or the signal's hit rate drops below 45% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a robust labor market, reducing recession probabilities and supporting higher equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a baseline market-cap weighted return benchmark by at least 1.5% per quarter, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Slowing real GDP growth concurrent with peaking housing permits signals a decelerating economic cycle, prompting investors to rotate out of cyclical equities and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 2.0%
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the predictive Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all out-of-sample test periods.
